messages.content: Build a branded `UserId` type in Effect v3 using Brand.nominal, and show a function signature that only accepts the branded form.
What's the Effect v3 way to declare a runtime-validated branded `Email`? Show a minimal example.
Build a branded `PositiveInt` in Effect v3 — refine numbers greater than zero, produce a `Brand.BrandErrors` on failure.
Explain briefly how branded types prevent primitive confusion in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation with `Brand.nominal`.
Implement a branded `NonEmptyString` in Effect v3 with a validating constructor. Strict types, no `any`.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of `type Email = string & { readonly _brand: 'Email' }` with an unsafe cast helper. Brief example.
Build a branded `Url` in Effect v3 using Brand.refined that rejects strings which don't parse as a URL.
Implement a branded `Port` number (1..65535) in Effect v3. All types declared.
Build a branded `Uuid` in Effect v3 with a regex-validated constructor, and a function that takes `Uuid` (not `string`).
Write an Effect v3 snippet that brands two distinct identifiers — `CustomerId` and `OrderId` — so they cannot be confused at call sites.
How do I brand a `Currency` amount (non-negative integer cents) in Effect v3? Minimal example.
Refactor this v3 code to use a branded `Slug` instead of `string`: `declare const open: (slug: string) => Effect.Effect<Page, Error>;`
Build a branded `Iso8601Date` in Effect v3 — string that round-trips through `Date.parse`.
Show me the Effect v3 pattern for combining two brands on the same type — e.g. `PositiveInt & UserId`. Brief example.
Convert this hand-rolled branded type to Effect v3 idioms: `type Tenant = string & { readonly __tenant: unique symbol };`
Implement a branded `SemVer` type in Effect v3 that refines strings of the form `major.minor.patch`.
Add a branded `AdminToken` type to this Effect v3 snippet so that the admin API can never be called with a plain string: `declare const deleteAll: (token: string) => Effect.Effect<void, Error>;`
Build an Effect v3 branded `Percentage` type (0..1 inclusive) with a validating constructor.
Explain briefly how `Brand.nominal` differs from `Brand.refined` in Effect v3, then show both side by side.
Write an Effect v3 snippet with a branded `Latitude` and `Longitude` pair, a `Coord` tuple type, and a distance function that accepts only branded values.
Write an fp-ts v2 validator that accumulates ALL errors when checking a user form (name, email, age). Return a `ReadonlyNonEmptyArray` of errors on failure.
What's the Effect v3 way to validate a payload and accumulate ALL errors instead of short-circuiting? Show a minimal example.
Build a validation-accumulating pipeline for a credit-card form (number, cvv, expiry) in Effect v3. Do not short-circuit on first error.
Write a fp-ts v2 applicative-validation pipeline for a registration form with 4 fields. All errors must accumulate.
How do I validate an array of items and collect every per-item error in Effect v3 without short-circuiting?
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of fp-ts `getApplicativeValidation` — accumulate all errors when validating a record. Brief example.
Explain briefly how validation accumulation differs from `E.chain` in fp-ts, then show a validated tuple using `sequenceT`.
Write an Effect v3 snippet that validates a config object with 5 fields and accumulates every failure.
Build a fp-ts v2 validation pipeline that parses an address (street, city, zip, country) and accumulates errors.
How do I accumulate validation errors from a list of Effect validators with a fixed concurrency limit in Effect v3?
Write an fp-ts v2 validator for a password that checks multiple rules (length, has digit, has uppercase, has special). Every failure must appear.
Show me the Effect v3 way to validate and decode a JSON payload, collecting all schema errors at once.
Implement an Effect v3 validator that checks three business rules on an order (total > 0, itemCount > 0, currency in set) and accumulates all violations.
Convert this fp-ts fail-fast validator to the accumulating-validation form: `pipe(vA(a), E.chain(vB), E.chain(vC))`.
Build an Effect v3 validator that runs 6 independent checks on a search query and accumulates every failure.
Refactor this v3 validator to use `Effect.partition` so every per-item error is preserved: `Effect.forEach(items, validate)`.
Explain briefly why `Effect.all(_, { mode: "validate" })` differs from the default mode, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 snippet that validates three dependent fields where each validator is independent (parallel) and accumulate errors.
What's the fp-ts v2 way to combine two Validations with a custom semigroup (not NonEmptyArray)? Brief example.
Add parallel error accumulation to this Effect v3 snippet: `Effect.all([vA, vB, vC])`.
